customers.name: Product Engineering Manager
customers.description: Leader of one or more stream-aligned product teams accountable for shipping customer-facing software, sustaining team health, and improving delivery reliability without expanding operational toil.
careAbout: Fast team onboarding and service setup
Predictable release flow without ticket-driven infrastructure work
Clear security and compliance defaults that do not block delivery
Reliable production operations with fewer manual interventions
Developer productivity improvements that are visible in team KPIs
winsThem: A self-service platform that turns common setup work into minutes instead of weeks
Opinionated golden paths for services, environments, delivery, and observability
Guardrails that are automated in the workflow instead of handled through slow reviews
Clear runtime ownership boundaries and support escalation paths
Evidence that the platform reduces lead time, change failure, and cognitive load
theirFear: A platform that standardizes the wrong things and slows teams down
Hidden platform complexity that forces teams to file tickets anyway
Security and reliability gaps caused by inconsistent service setup
Losing autonomy because every deviation requires platform negotiation

jobsToBeDone.name: Launch a product team on the platform without bespoke setup work
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Move a team from idea or legacy service state to a paved-road delivery model with standard templates, environments, pipelines, secrets, observability, and support guidance.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: New or modernized product teams can ship safely on a common platform foundation with much less bootstrap effort and fewer local platform decisions.

steps.step: Create the service skeleton and ownership model
steps.description: Start with a supported template, clear ownership metadata, and standard runtime defaults.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Developer Portal and Service Catalog
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides one entry point for templates, ownership metadata, documentation, and platform workflows.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Golden Path Templates
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Offers opinionated starting points for APIs, workers, web apps, and event-driven services.

steps.description: Create non-production and production-ready environments, delivery pipelines, and release controls without custom ticket chains.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Environment Provisioning and Ephemeral Environments
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Automates environment creation, teardown, and policy-aware runtime setup.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Delivery Pipelines and Release Automation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides reusable build, test, deploy, rollback, and promotion workflows.

media.title: Product Engineering Manager JTBD step 2: Provision environments and delivery workflows
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Launch a product team on the platform without bespoke setup work": Provision environments and delivery workflows.

steps.step: Activate security, telemetry, and support paths
steps.description: Apply secrets, identity, policy, logs, alerts, dashboards, and production support conventions by default.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Identity, Secrets and Access Guardrails
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Standardizes access, secret delivery, and workload identity before teams ship.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Observability and Telemetry Foundation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Bootstraps logging, metrics, tracing, dashboards, and on-call hooks into the service from day one.

media.title: Product Engineering Manager JTBD step 3: Activate security, telemetry, and support paths
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Launch a product team on the platform without bespoke setup work": Activate security, telemetry, and support paths.

jobsToBeDone.name: Keep delivery fast while platform standards tighten
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Balance speed, autonomy, governance, and reliability as the organization raises the bar for software supply chain, compliance, and production excellence.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Teams keep shipping value while more of the delivery system becomes standardized, auditable, and automated.

steps.step: Measure where platform friction actually exists
steps.description: Use workflow telemetry and service adoption data to find where teams still wait, hand off, or work around the platform.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Developer Experience and Workflow Telemetry
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows onboarding time, self-service success, lead time, and adoption drop-offs.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Cost, Capacity and Usage Insights
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Exposes where teams pay for inefficiency, idle environments, and expensive patterns.

media.title: Product Engineering Manager JTBD step 1: Measure where platform friction actually exists
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Keep delivery fast while platform standards tighten": Measure where platform friction actually exists.

steps.step: Automate the controls that teams repeatedly face
steps.description: Push security and compliance checks into templates and pipelines instead of governance meetings and manual reviews.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Policy as Code and Compliance Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Encodes required checks and exception workflows into the delivery path.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Artifact Supply Chain and Base Images
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps runtime packages, dependencies, and provenance standards consistent.

media.title: Product Engineering Manager JTBD step 2: Automate the controls that teams repeatedly face
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Keep delivery fast while platform standards tighten": Automate the controls that teams repeatedly face.

steps.step: Recover quickly when shared foundations fail
steps.description: Handle platform incidents and rollback needs without forcing every team to improvise operations locally.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Reliability Workflows and Incident Automation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Coordinates incident response, rollback, runbooks, and shared escalation flows.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Runtime Connectivity and Integration Patterns
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides approved patterns for network access, service-to-service communication, and dependency management.

top.name: Developer flow and adoption
top.description: Shows whether product teams can onboard and use the platform with less friction.

branches.name: Onboarding speed
branches.description: Measures how quickly teams become productive on the platform.

children.name: Median team onboarding time
children.description: Median elapsed time from onboarding start to a usable platform setup.

children.name: Self-service completion rate
children.description: Share of core onboarding and setup workflows completed without manual support.

branches.name: Delivery confidence
branches.description: Shows whether the standard path improves team delivery outcomes.

children.name: Lead time for change
children.description: Elapsed time from code committed to production successfully.

children.name: Change failure rate
children.description: Share of releases that cause rollback, incident, or urgent fix work.

top.name: Platform leverage
top.description: Shows whether the platform creates enough organizational value to justify its investment.

branches.name: Adoption leverage
branches.description: Measures whether product teams actually move onto the platform.

children.name: Platform adoption across product teams
children.description: Share of mainstream product teams using the supported platform path.

children.name: Platform support tickets per onboarding
children.description: Manual support workload needed to onboard a team.

branches.name: Engineering productivity
branches.description: Measures whether platform investment frees up engineering capacity.

children.name: Engineering capacity redirected from setup and support work
children.description: Estimated engineering time moved from repetitive setup to product delivery.

productStrategy.vision: Every product team should be able to start, ship, and operate services on a trusted platform without becoming experts in cloud plumbing, security internals, or bespoke delivery tooling.

1_year.focus: Make the platform the default path for new services and the easiest path for mainstream product teams.
1_year.customerKPI: Median team onboarding time under 2 days
1_year.businessKPI: Lead time for change improves across platform-adopting teams
milestones: Golden-path service templates cover the most common service types
Platform onboarding is documented, measurable, and largely self-service
Standard observability and secret-management flows are active by default

3_year.focus: Reduce ticket-based platform work and make guardrails part of the delivery system itself.
3_year.businessKPI: Change failure rate reduced across product teams using the platform
milestones: Policy-as-code replaces most environment and release approvals
Runtime and pipeline exceptions are managed through explicit product workflows
Cross-team delivery telemetry informs platform investment decisions

5_year.focus: Operate a platform portfolio that spans application, data, and reliability foundations with clear product boundaries.
5_year.productTheme: Platform portfolio and internal product maturity
5_year.customerKPI: Developer satisfaction with platform experience above 8/10
5_year.businessKPI: Engineering capacity redirected from repetitive setup and support to product delivery
milestones: Platform products have explicit service levels, customer research cadence, and lifecycle management
Legacy delivery and runtime paths are retired or isolated
Platform investment is governed by measurable customer and business outcomes

customerJourneyStories.name: Launch a product team on the platform without bespoke setup work
customerJourneyStories.summary: Product Engineering Manager moves from recognizing that a platform that standardizes the wrong things and slows teams down to repeatedly achieving new or modernized product teams can ship safely on a common platform foundation with much less bootstrap effort and fewer local platform decisions through a journey that proves a self-service platform that turns common setup work into minutes instead of weeks and protects fast team onboarding and service setup.

stages.narrative: The journey starts when Product Engineering Manager sees that a platform that standardizes the wrong things and slows teams down is no longer acceptable, or when the current way of working can no longer protect fast team onboarding and service setup.

Discovery happens when the offering shows up with proof points that connect directly to launch a product team on the platform without bespoke setup work and signal a self-service platform that turns common setup work into minutes instead of weeks.

Product Engineering Manager evaluates whether the solution is relevant, easy to understand, and credible enough to improve fast team onboarding and service setup without creating hidden platform complexity that forces teams to file tickets anyway.

The trial succeeds when Product Engineering Manager can quickly experience create the service skeleton and ownership model and provision environments and delivery workflows with limited friction and can see early evidence that move a team from idea or legacy service state to a paved-road delivery model with standard templates, environments, pipelines, secrets, observability, and support guidance will work in the real environment.

Engagement deepens as Product Engineering Manager uses the product to make activate security, telemetry, and support paths part of normal operations, turning the initial promise into dependable day-to-day value anchored in opinionated golden paths for services, environments, delivery, and observability.

Retention comes when the product keeps making value visible through developer flow and adoption, the product still supports the next priority, "Keep delivery fast while platform standards tighten", and gives Product Engineering Manager confidence that staying is safer than switching back or moving elsewhere.

customers.name: Software Engineer
customers.description: Day-to-day engineer on a stream-aligned product team who needs the platform to make common engineering work faster, clearer, and less dependent on hidden infrastructure knowledge.
careAbout: Starting new services and changes quickly without platform tickets
Clear local development, test, and deployment workflows
Reliable defaults for secrets, permissions, and environments
Fast feedback from pipelines and platform tooling
Enough observability to debug and recover without guesswork
winsThem: Golden paths that work for the normal case without extra negotiation
One place to find docs, ownership, dependencies, and supported commands
Preview and runtime workflows that behave predictably
Errors and policy failures that are understandable and actionable
Fast incident and support escalation when the shared platform breaks
theirFear: Needing tribal knowledge to complete routine engineering tasks
Blocked delivery because the paved road is incomplete or confusing
Debugging failures hidden behind opaque platform abstractions
Platform standards that add steps without removing real work

jobsToBeDone.name: Ship everyday changes through the platform without friction
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Use the supported path for common coding, testing, deployment, and debugging work without needing deep platform specialization.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Everyday engineering work moves faster, with fewer blocked handoffs and less bespoke setup or debugging toil.

capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides a usable default starting point for common service and delivery tasks.

capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides predictable runtime environments without waiting on manual setup.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Delivery Pipelines and Release Automation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides fast feedback and a standard release path for common changes.

productStrategy.vision: A software engineer should be able to use the platform as a reliable everyday engineering tool, not as a separate specialization they have to learn around their real job.

1_year.focus: Make the most common engineering workflows fast, understandable, and supportable.
1_year.customerKPI: Time from repo creation to first production deployment under 1 week
1_year.businessKPI: Lead time for change improves across platform-adopting teams
milestones: Common workflows are documented and self-service
Pipeline and runtime failures are more understandable
Debugging and escalation paths are explicit

3_year.focus: Reduce daily platform friction enough that local workarounds become the exception.
3_year.businessKPI: Platform support load per active team materially reduced
milestones: Most high-frequency support themes are productized away
Engineers can diagnose common failures without tribal knowledge
Preview and runtime workflows are stable and trusted

5_year.focus: Make the platform feel like part of normal software engineering, not a central platform hurdle.
5_year.customerKPI: Developer satisfaction with platform experience above 8/10
5_year.businessKPI: Engineering throughput improves without proportional platform support growth
milestones: The standard path is the default for mainstream engineering work
Most recurrent platform friction is visible and measurable
Platform product decisions are grounded in daily engineer workflows

customerJourneyStories.name: Ship everyday changes through the platform without friction
customerJourneyStories.summary: Software Engineer moves from recognizing that needing tribal knowledge to complete routine engineering tasks to repeatedly achieving everyday engineering work moves faster, with fewer blocked handoffs and less bespoke setup or debugging toil through a journey that proves golden paths that work for the normal case without extra negotiation and protects starting new services and changes quickly without platform tickets.

stages.narrative: The journey starts when Software Engineer sees that needing tribal knowledge to complete routine engineering tasks is no longer acceptable, or when the current way of working can no longer protect starting new services and changes quickly without platform tickets.

Discovery happens when the offering shows up with proof points that connect directly to ship everyday changes through the platform without friction and signal golden paths that work for the normal case without extra negotiation.

Software Engineer evaluates whether the solution is relevant, easy to understand, and credible enough to improve starting new services and changes quickly without platform tickets without creating blocked delivery because the paved road is incomplete or confusing.

The trial succeeds when Software Engineer can quickly experience start from the supported path and run, test, and deploy with confidence with limited friction and can see early evidence that use the supported path for common coding, testing, deployment, and debugging work without needing deep platform specialization will work in the real environment.

Engagement deepens as Software Engineer uses the product to make debug and recover quickly part of normal operations, turning the initial promise into dependable day-to-day value anchored in one place to find docs, ownership, dependencies, and supported commands.

Retention comes when the product keeps making value visible through everyday developer flow, the product continues to make ship everyday changes through the platform without friction easier and more dependable over time, and gives Software Engineer confidence that staying is safer than switching back or moving elsewhere.

customers.name: Staff Software Engineer / Tech Lead
customers.description: Senior engineer responsible for technical direction within a stream-aligned team, balancing local product needs with platform standards, runtime reliability, and long-term architecture quality.
careAbout: Strong default engineering standards without unnecessary abstraction
Fast local iteration on top of common building blocks
Clear extension points and escape hatches when the paved road does not fit
Production visibility, rollback confidence, and debuggability
Low cognitive load for cross-cutting infrastructure and compliance topics
winsThem: Reusable templates, pipelines, and runtime integrations that stay transparent
Good local docs, examples, and support channels
Consistent environments that reduce surprise between dev, test, and prod
Reliable telemetry and incident tooling built into the platform
theirFear: Black-box platform abstractions that hide real system behavior
Forced standardization that blocks legitimate product-specific needs
Platform coupling that makes debugging and migration harder

jobsToBeDone.name: Ship services on a paved road without losing engineering judgment
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Adopt platform standards where they create leverage while still understanding the technical behavior, limits, and integration surfaces of the system.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Teams move faster on common paths, deviate only when necessary, and keep architecture quality visible.

productStrategy.vision: Senior engineers should spend their time on product architecture and technical leadership, not on rebuilding platform plumbing team by team.

1_year.focus: Make supported service patterns obvious, well-documented, and operationally trustworthy.
1_year.customerKPI: Time from repo creation to first production deployment under 1 week
1_year.businessKPI: More product teams migrate off bespoke delivery setups
milestones: Portal documents supported service types and extension guidance
Common runtime and delivery integrations are one-command defaults
Platform incidents have clearer ownership and response flows

3_year.focus: Standardize the 80 percent path while keeping principled escape hatches.
3_year.customerKPI: Reduction in local bespoke pipeline maintenance
3_year.businessKPI: Lower platform support load per engineering team
milestones: Platform APIs and contracts are versioned and discoverable
Exception handling is explicit, governed, and fast enough to use
Runtime telemetry is consistent across the service estate

5_year.focus: Let platform and application architecture evolve independently through strong contracts and internal product management.
5_year.customerKPI: Architectural rework from platform changes materially reduced
5_year.businessKPI: Higher engineering throughput with lower operational variance
milestones: Platform upgrades are managed as product migrations rather than ad hoc requests
Observability and reliability posture is comparable across product areas
Platform evolution uses usage and productivity evidence, not opinion alone

customerJourneyStories.name: Ship services on a paved road without losing engineering judgment
customerJourneyStories.summary: Staff Software Engineer / Tech Lead moves from recognizing that black-box platform abstractions that hide real system behavior to repeatedly achieving teams move faster on common paths, deviate only when necessary, and keep architecture quality visible through a journey that proves reusable templates, pipelines, and runtime integrations that stay transparent and protects strong default engineering standards without unnecessary abstraction.

stages.narrative: The journey starts when Staff Software Engineer / Tech Lead sees that black-box platform abstractions that hide real system behavior is no longer acceptable, or when the current way of working can no longer protect strong default engineering standards without unnecessary abstraction.

Discovery happens when the offering shows up with proof points that connect directly to ship services on a paved road without losing engineering judgment and signal reusable templates, pipelines, and runtime integrations that stay transparent.

Staff Software Engineer / Tech Lead evaluates whether the solution is relevant, easy to understand, and credible enough to improve strong default engineering standards without unnecessary abstraction without creating forced standardization that blocks legitimate product-specific needs.

The trial succeeds when Staff Software Engineer / Tech Lead can quickly experience choose the closest supported architecture pattern and integrate with the runtime and platform services with limited friction and can see early evidence that adopt platform standards where they create leverage while still understanding the technical behavior, limits, and integration surfaces of the system will work in the real environment.

Engagement deepens as Staff Software Engineer / Tech Lead uses the product to make operate the service with confidence part of normal operations, turning the initial promise into dependable day-to-day value anchored in good local docs, examples, and support channels.

Retention comes when the product keeps making value visible through transparent engineering acceleration, the product continues to make ship services on a paved road without losing engineering judgment easier and more dependable over time, and gives Staff Software Engineer / Tech Lead confidence that staying is safer than switching back or moving elsewhere.

group: Risk, Reliability and Specialist Engineering Partners

customers.name: Site Reliability and Security Lead
customers.description: Leader responsible for production resilience, operational controls, and security posture across many product teams. Needs platform engineering to turn reliability and governance into repeatable defaults instead of ticket queues.
careAbout: Consistent controls across teams and environments
Fast incident response with shared telemetry and runbooks
Reducing misconfiguration and uncontrolled platform drift
Auditability without slowing delivery to a halt
winsThem: Security and reliability standards embedded in templates, runtime policies, and delivery automation
A service catalog with ownership and dependency visibility
Shared incident tooling and observability signals across teams
theirFear: Security reviews that arrive too late in the workflow

jobsToBeDone.name: Raise the engineering control baseline without becoming a central bottleneck
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Move governance, reliability, and operational discipline into the platform so teams can comply by following the normal path.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher baseline resilience and control coverage with less manual review overhead.

steps.description: Translate security, identity, reliability, and audit requirements into platform-managed standards.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Policy as Code and Compliance Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Turns policies into enforceable checks, exceptions, and evidence.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Identity, Secrets and Access Guardrails
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Standardizes the most sensitive access and secret-management paths.

media.title: Site Reliability and Security Lead JTBD step 1: Define the non-negotiable controls
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Raise the engineering control baseline without becoming a central bottleneck": Define the non-negotiable controls.

steps.step: Detect and respond to production risk quickly
steps.description: Use shared telemetry, incident automation, and service ownership data to coordinate response.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Observability and Telemetry Foundation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Creates a common signal model across product teams.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Reliability Workflows and Incident Automation
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Coordinates severity handling, response orchestration, and post-incident follow-up.

media.title: Site Reliability and Security Lead JTBD step 2: Detect and respond to production risk quickly
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Raise the engineering control baseline without becoming a central bottleneck": Detect and respond to production risk quickly.

children.name: Production services with standard telemetry

branches.name: Operational resilience
branches.description: Measures whether response to shared failures is fast and consistent.

children.name: Incident MTTR for common platform failure classes
children.description: Mean time to recover from recurring shared-platform incident types.

children.name: Exception review cycle time
children.description: Elapsed time to review and resolve standard exception requests.

top.name: Scalable governance and resilience
top.description: Shows whether controls and incident discipline reduce central manual work and preventable failures.

branches.name: Risk reduction
branches.description: Measures whether stronger defaults reduce avoidable incidents.

children.name: Fewer Sev1 and Sev2 incidents caused by preventable platform variance
children.description: Count of major incidents caused by missing or inconsistent platform baselines.

children.name: Change failure rate
children.description: Share of changes that cause rollback, incident, or urgent fix work.

branches.name: Governance efficiency
branches.description: Measures whether automation replaces manual governance effort.

children.name: Manual governance workload
children.description: Time central teams spend on repetitive policy and control review work.

children.name: Audit and incident follow-up effort reduced through automation
children.description: Central-team effort saved through automated evidence and response workflows.

productStrategy.vision: Security and reliability should be easiest to achieve through the normal platform path, not through slow parallel governance mechanisms.

1_year.focus: Make the standard path compliant and observable by default.
1_year.customerKPI: Production services with standard telemetry and workload identity above 75%
1_year.businessKPI: Audit and incident follow-up effort reduced through automation
milestones: Baseline policy checks run in all standard delivery pipelines
Service ownership and dependency data is discoverable
Shared incident response workflows are in regular use

3_year.focus: Manage exceptions and risk using platform evidence rather than manual spreadsheets.
3_year.customerKPI: Exception review cycle time under 5 business days
3_year.businessKPI: Fewer Sev1 and Sev2 incidents caused by preventable platform variance
milestones: Policy exceptions are versioned, time-bound, and traceable
Control coverage is reported through platform telemetry
Post-incident platform fixes feed templates and pipelines automatically

5_year.focus: Use platform architecture to keep reliability and governance scalable as the estate grows.
5_year.customerKPI: Most control evidence produced automatically from platform workflows
5_year.businessKPI: Central reliability and security teams spend more time on systemic improvement than ticket triage
milestones: Control evidence and runtime posture are continuously available
Most high-frequency operational failures are automated away or caught pre-production
Platform and reliability roadmaps are tightly linked through shared telemetry

customerJourneyStories.name: Raise the engineering control baseline without becoming a central bottleneck
customerJourneyStories.summary: Site Reliability and Security Lead moves from recognizing that shadow infrastructure and ungoverned exceptions to repeatedly achieving higher baseline resilience and control coverage with less manual review overhead through a journey that proves security and reliability standards embedded in templates, runtime policies, and delivery automation and protects consistent controls across teams and environments.

stages.narrative: The journey starts when Site Reliability and Security Lead sees that shadow infrastructure and ungoverned exceptions is no longer acceptable, or when the current way of working can no longer protect consistent controls across teams and environments.

Discovery happens when the offering shows up with proof points that connect directly to raise the engineering control baseline without becoming a central bottleneck and signal security and reliability standards embedded in templates, runtime policies, and delivery automation.

Site Reliability and Security Lead evaluates whether the solution is relevant, easy to understand, and credible enough to improve consistent controls across teams and environments without creating incidents caused by inconsistent baselines.

The trial succeeds when Site Reliability and Security Lead can quickly experience define the non-negotiable controls and detect and respond to production risk quickly with limited friction and can see early evidence that move governance, reliability, and operational discipline into the platform so teams can comply by following the normal path will work in the real environment.

Engagement deepens as Site Reliability and Security Lead uses the product to make higher baseline resilience and control coverage with less manual review overhead part of normal operations, turning the initial promise into dependable day-to-day value anchored in a service catalog with ownership and dependency visibility.

Retention comes when the product keeps making value visible through reliable shared controls, the product continues to make raise the engineering control baseline without becoming a central bottleneck easier and more dependable over time, and gives Site Reliability and Security Lead confidence that staying is safer than switching back or moving elsewhere.

customers.name: Data and ML Platform Lead
customers.description: Leader of a specialist engineering platform area that depends on shared runtime, identity, delivery, and observability foundations while maintaining additional workload and tooling needs of its own.
careAbout: Being able to build on shared platform capabilities instead of rebuilding commodity foundations
Clear boundaries between the general application platform and specialist workload needs
Reliable access to secure compute, storage, and telemetry patterns
winsThem: A modular platform portfolio with shared contracts for identity, deployment, and observability
Extension points for specialist workloads without forking the whole platform
theirFear: A one-size-fits-all platform that ignores specialist workloads
Duplicated foundation work across platform teams
Unclear ownership between central and specialist platform groups

jobsToBeDone.name: Build specialist platform capabilities on top of shared foundations
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Reuse the central platform where leverage is high while extending it for data, ML, and workflow-specific needs.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Specialist platform teams move faster and stay aligned with enterprise standards without losing their domain-specific capabilities.

productStrategy.vision: Platform engineering should behave as a portfolio of internal products with a shared foundation and explicit boundaries, not as one giant platform that every specialist team has to bend around.

1_year.focus: Create clean contracts between the core application platform and specialist platform domains.
1_year.customerKPI: Shared-foundation reuse across specialist platform teams increases
1_year.businessKPI: Less duplicate engineering spend on common platform services
milestones: Portal documents shared contracts and ownership boundaries
Specialist teams reuse the common CI/CD, identity, and telemetry foundations
Exception patterns are reviewed as product decisions rather than local hacks

3_year.focus: Treat shared capabilities as durable internal products with versioned contracts and service levels.
3_year.customerKPI: Fewer duplicated custom platform components across engineering
3_year.businessKPI: Platform investment prioritization improves through portfolio governance
milestones: Cross-platform dependencies are discoverable and measured
Shared platform capabilities publish lifecycle states and migration paths
Portfolio decisions use adoption, reliability, and leverage evidence

5_year.focus: Run a coherent internal platform portfolio that scales to new engineering modes without rebuilding the basics.
5_year.customerKPI: Time to launch new specialist platform capabilities materially reduced
5_year.businessKPI: Higher leverage from the central platform team across the engineering organization
milestones: Core platform services are composable enough to support new specialist domains
Platform contracts survive large organizational change without chaos
Internal product management governs platform lifecycle and investment
